description: On reconnect the client resubscribes fresh with no cursor, silently missing every message sent during the disconnect.
body: Failure mode: a dropped connection reconnects and starts receiving new messages, but nothing sent during the gap is replayed, leaving the client permanently behind the server's state. Detection signals: no sequence ids or client cursor; reconnect path does not request a backfill or snapshot; users report missing updates after brief network blips. Do not treat reconnect as continuity. Safer fallback: assign monotonic ids, persist the last-seen id on the client, and replay or snapshot from it on reconnect with idempotent apply.
